summary: DHH responds to excitement around PHP 5's new SimpleXML features by suggesting the enthusiasm is misplaced. He notes that Ruby already handles XML processing elegantly, implying that PHP is just catching up to capabilities Ruby developers already enjoy. He forgives the Zend creators' pride in their work but finds it harder to understand why Java developers like Russell Beattie would get excited about PHP 5's XML handling when better alternatives exist.
key_points: PHP 5's SimpleXML was being heralded as revolutionary, but Ruby already had comparable XML processing capabilities
DHH distinguishes between creators being proud of their work (forgivable) and outsiders hyping inferior solutions (less forgivable)
The post reflects the early-2000s language wars and DHH's advocacy for Ruby over PHP and Java
money_quotes: As a Ruby developer, the tune of all these trumpets heralding the coming of SimpleXML in PHP5 sounds a bit out of key.
I can forgive their overreaching enthusiasm, though, they're just really proud of their creation.
key_insight: DHH positions Ruby as already superior to PHP for XML processing, framing PHP 5's celebrated new features as old news in the Ruby world.
